strengths: Strong Power BI experience with dashboard project
Solid SQL and Python data engineering skills
Hands-on ELT/ETL with Airflow, dbt, BigQuery
Proven BI impact with quantified outcomes
CI/CD and data quality automation experience
weaknesses: No Databricks or Delta tables listed
No Kafka or streaming ingestion experience
Not locally based in NJ (resume lists NY)
improvement_bullets: Extracted and transformed data from REST APIs and relational databases, building structured, clean datasets using Python and SQL, which directly supported front-end feature development and reduced UI bugs, and can be adapted for Spark processing and Databricks Delta tables.

Modeled raw API data into a dbt star schema, optimizing for BigQuery partitioning and clustering, and I can leverage this experience with Databricks optimizations and Delta tables to enhance performance.

Built a Power BI dashboard correlating Fed rate decisions, inflation, and S&P 500 performance across 5 years of FRED data, demonstrating strong hands-on expertise with Power BI including data modeling and dashboard design, and can implement DAX measures to further refine insights.

Designed an interactive Tableau dashboard enabling self-service analysis for non-technical stakeholders, visualizing 2021 NYC arrest trends by time, location, and demographics, and I can apply similar principles to develop robust Power BI dashboards for project management and product data reporting.

Automated data quality validation with Great Expectations and deployed a CI/CD pipeline via GitHub Actions, and I am eager to apply this proficiency to manage data pipelines with streaming ingestion and batch processing, potentially using Kafka.
cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am excited to submit my application for the AIML Data Engineer opportunity with CloudIngest. I am interested in this opportunity because the role's focus on bridging data engineering with business intelligence to drive actionable insights through scalable data pipelines and intuitive dashboards aligns perfectly with my career aspirations and proven skillset. What interests me about working for CloudIngest is your commitment to leveraging data to empower internal project management and product data initiatives, which excites me because I thrive on building solutions that directly impact business outcomes.

I believe my expertise in data pipelines and strong Power BI skills would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on efficiency and impactful results. In fact, at Shine Masters Cleaning Services, I built a business intelligence reporting system in Excel that identified inefficiencies, driving a 40% improvement in operational efficiency. This resulted in freeing team capacity for higher-value work and increasing leads by 40% through strategic marketing data analysis, showcasing my ability to translate data into tangible business improvements.

Ultimately, I know that the AIML Data Engineer role at CloudIngest would allow me to bring my data pipeline development expertise to designing, building, and optimizing data pipelines to support AI/ML models and analytics. Additionally, my project experience as a Data Analytics Fellow enabled me to develop my data visualization and reporting skills. Specifically, for the FRED Economic Batch Pipeline project, I built a production-grade ELT pipeline ingesting Federal Reserve economic data into Google BigQuery using Python, Airflow, dbt, and GCS, and then modeled the data into a dbt star schema. This project resulted in reduced analyst query time via BigQuery partitioning and clustering, and demonstrated my ability to deliver efficient and well-documented data solutions.

I'm excited by the opportunity to use my strong Power BI skills in working on developing and maintaining Power BI dashboards for project management and product data reporting at CloudIngest. I know that my experience with data engineering best practices and business intelligence reporting aligns with what it takes to succeed in the AIML Data Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail and explain how my background, including my adaptability to learn new technologies like Spark and Kafka, can benefit your team.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Warm regards,
Justin Miller
